mnemonics: B.O.O.T. (Bagging Of Overfitting Trees): Bagging many trees helps Overcome Overfitting by reducing variance through Tree averaging.
keyPoints: Random Forests use bagging (bootstrap aggregating) to create multiple decision trees from random subsets of the data.
They introduce additional randomness by selecting a random subset of features for each split, decorrelating the trees.
The final prediction is made by averaging (regression) or taking a majority vote (classification) across all trees.
commonMistakes: Not tuning hyperparameters like n_estimators (number of trees) and max_features (number of features considered for a split).
Assuming that because it's an ensemble method, it is immune to overfitting. It can still overfit on noisy data.
